question: Write a short story about a time traveler in ancient Egypt.
answer:
The chronometer on Elias’s wrist didn’t beep; it screamed. A high-pitched whine that vibrated in his teeth before the world dissolved into a nauseating slurry of grey static.

When the static cleared, the smell hit him first. Not the sterile, recycled air of the 22nd-century lab, but a thick, organic cocktail of river mud, roasting meat, and unwashed humanity.

Elias stumbled, his boots sinking into soft earth. He checked his wrist. *3050 BCE. Giza Plateau. Target: The Great Pyramid, Pre-Constru
